stories.title: CNN correspondent shares his takeaways from his recent visit to Iran
stories.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks CNN's Fred Pleitgen for his takeaways from his recent reporting trip to Iran.

stories.title: Exiled Iranians watch the war from afar, wondering what awaits when it ends
stories.description: Iranians who fled the country before the war with the U.S. and Israel are now watching it unfold, wondering what will happen when it ends.

stories.title: Despite Trump's frustration, Spain's Pedro Sánchez doubles down on opposing the war
stories.description: President Trump has been voicing his frustration with the Spanish government's opposition to the war in Iran. But far from backing down, Spain's prime minister is happy to walk into the spotlight.

stories.title: Khameni's killing raises old questions about U.S. assassinating foreign leaders
stories.description: The killing of Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei raises questions about whether the U.S. as a democracy should be in the business of assassinating foreign leaders.

stories.title: Fear of Iranian mines in the Strait of Hormuz could further slow the flow of oil
stories.description: Attacks by Iran have already nearly halted the flow of oil through the vital waterway as commercial ship crews fear being hit by missiles, drones or mines.

stories.title: 'There's no hiding place on a ship': The sailors stranded near Iran
stories.description: Drones, cruise missiles and fighter jets have become a common sight for many sailors stranded on ships in the Gulf.

stories.title: Bodies of 84 Iranian sailors killed in US torpedo strike to be repatriated
stories.description: The seamen died when Iranian warship the Iris Dena was sunk on 4 March by a US submarine near Sri Lanka.

stories.title: 'Follow the smoke': BBC spends day with emergency teams as Israel strikes south Lebanon
stories.description: The BBC visited the city of Nabatieh, witnessing the pace and scale of Israel's bombing as its war with Hezbollah continues to escalate.

stories.title: Mission accomplished? The 2003 boast that haunts today's Iran conflict
stories.description: The echoes between the conflicts are certainly there but there are also profound differences.

stories.title: Iran's new supreme leader vows to block Strait of Hormuz in first statement
stories.description: A message attributed to Mojtaba Khamenei also says Iran will continue targeting US bases in the region.

stories.title: Tracking the wave of ship attacks that has choked off Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Six ships have been reportedly attacked in the Gulf in less than 48 hours, bringing the total vessels attacked during the war to 18.

stories.title: Emirati minister tells BBC Iran must end strikes on Gulf
stories.description: The UAE says more than 1,800 drones and missiles have been projected at the country since the war began.

stories.title: 'What if we're left with ruins?': Doubts creep in for Iranians who supported war
stories.description: Teachers, engineers and shopkeepers tell the BBC they fear Iran falling into chaos.

stories.title: Israel pounds Beirut suburbs and south Lebanon after Hezbollah rocket barrage
stories.description: Some Israeli strikes also hit other parts of the Lebanese capital, with 12 people reportedly killed along the seafront.

stories.title: How Iran war laid bare the world's reliance on Gulf oil and gas
stories.description: Countries around the world are feeling the impact of the conflict and the resulting energy price shock.

stories.title: British man charged in Dubai for alleged filming of Iranian missiles
stories.description: Foreign Office officials are understood to be supporting his family.

stories.title: 'Fingers on the trigger': Deadly warnings for Iranians being urged to take action
stories.description: Iran's police chief has warned that his forces would treat anyone taking to the streets "at the enemy's request" as an enemy.

stories.title: Questions mount for Hegseth over possible US involvement in strike on Iranian school
stories.description: Democrats have written to ask the defence secretary whether the US was responsible, but he says the issue is being investigated.

stories.title: Explosions reported on two foreign vessels in the Gulf, authorities say
stories.description: Pressure has intensified on the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's most important shipping lanes, since Israel and the US attacked Iran last month.

stories.title: EU and UK demand Israel stop surge in West Bank settler violence since Iran war
stories.description: Six Palestinians have been killed during attacks by settlers in the West Bank since the start of Israel's war, the UN says.

stories.title: Middle East war having 'huge impact' on EU economy: Commissioner Christophe Hansen
stories.description: It’s no secret that farmers across the EU have grown increasingly frustrated with the state of their industry. Profitability is falling, competition from abroad is fierce, and worries about paperwork and bureaucracy continue to mount. Fewer young people are taking over the family...

stories.title: Iran conflict: EU walks diplomatic tightrope between rule of law and support for democracy
stories.description: The EU is in a bind. EU institutions and many member states have condemned the Iranian government’s waves of repression against its own population. At the same time, the EU has said it would welcome a more democratic form of government in Iran.

stories.title: Iran's new supreme leader 'likely disfigured', US defence chief says
stories.description: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth said on Friday that Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei was wounded and "likely disfigured", casting doubt on his ability to lead after nearly two weeks of conflict.

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz: Iran leverages control of a critical chokepoint in the global oil trade
stories.description: Amid the current crisis surrounding the Strait of Hormuz, Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Dr. Guy Laron, Author of "Oil Wars", Historian and Senior Lecturer in International Relations at the Hebrew University of Jerusalem. According to Dr Laron, what we are witnessing is not m...

stories.title: War in Ukraine: Zelensky meets Macron for talks on Russia
stories.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky met French counterpart Emmanuel Macron in Paris on Friday for talks aimed at ramping up pressure on Russia to end its four-year invasion. The visit comes as Kyiv's allies worry that a temporary lifting of Russian oil sanctions by the United ...

stories.title: REPLAY: US Defense Secretary holds press conference on Iran war
stories.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says Iran’s new supreme leader “is wounded and likely disfigured.” Hegseth spoke Friday, a day after Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei released a written statement vowing to continue the war. It was Khamenei’s first statement since he ass...

stories.title: French officer killed in drone attack in Iraq
stories.description: A French officer was killed in a drone attack in Iraq, President Emmanuel Macron said on Friday, amid the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Earlier the Iran-backed Iraqi group, Ashab Alkahf, warned that French interests in the region were now targets after the arrival of a Fre...

stories.title: 'Mental, subjective impact': Iran taking on 'American might' with cyberterrorism
stories.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Farad Khajkvar, Director of Studies at the School of Higher Studies, EHESS, author of the forthcoming book in French, Iran: The End of Totalitarianism. He sees today’s Iran as a system in which religion has become subordinate to politics, and...

stories.title: War in the Middle East: New wave of attacks on Gulf states
stories.description: Iran launched multiple attacks early Friday on Gulf Arab states, including dozens of drones at Saudi Arabia, following warnings from its new supreme leader about hosting American bases, and US President Donald Trump threatened major new retaliation.

stories.title: French soldier killed in attack in Iraqi Kurdistan
stories.description: A French soldier was killed in an attack in Iraq's autonomous Kurdistan region, President Emmanuel Macron said on Friday, confirming the first French military death in the Middle East war. He did not say who was behind the attack. Since US-Israeli strikes on Iran last month engul...

stories.title: In first public comments, Iran’s supreme leader pledges to keep fighting
stories.description: Iran’s secretive new leader issued his first public statements Thursday, resolving to keep fighting, promising more pain for Gulf Arab states and threatening to open “other fronts” in a war that has already disrupted world energy supplies, the global economy and international tra...

stories.title: NOMA chef steps down after New York Times report of abuse
stories.description: PRESS REVIEW – Friday, March 13: We examine how the press is responding to Lebanon emerging as a new front in the US and Israel’s war against Iran. One paper describes the conflict in Lebanon as an extension of Israel’s war against Hezbollah. Also in the headlines: the chef and c...

stories.title: Middle East: Millions forced from their homes since the start of the war
stories.description: Up to 3.2 million people in Iran have been displaced since the Middle East war erupted, the United Nations refugee agency said Thursday and authorities in Lebanon say 800,000 have been forced from their homes as Israel’s military destroys buildings linked to Iran-backed Hezbollah...

stories.title: Zelensky arrives in Paris for talks on pressuring Russia
stories.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky arrived in Paris on Friday, his spokesman said, for talks with President Emmanuel Macron on ramping up pressure on Russia. The visit comes as talks brokered between Kyiv and Moscow by the United States to end the four-year invasion have been...

stories.title: US’s new scramble for Africa is biomedical imperialism
stories.description: From Zimbabwe to Zambia, US health deals are raising fears of a new era of biomedical extractivism.

stories.title: IEA announces release of 400 million barrels of oil. But is it enough?
stories.description: Global energy watchdog pledges largest release from oil reserves amid war on Iran. But is 'one-shot' tactic sufficient?

stories.title: What’s happened in Gaza and the West Bank since the start of the Iran war?
stories.description: Israel has closed the Rafah border crossing and Israeli settler attacks in the occupied West Bank have continued.

stories.title: Air attack strikes Tehran during al-Quds Day rally
stories.description: An air attack struck near thousands of Iranians marking al-Quds Day in Tehran, reportedly killing at least one person.

stories.title: Turkiye says NATO defences intercepted third missile from Iran
stories.description: The missiles increasingly pose a test for NATO member Turkiye and the alliance.

stories.title: Netanyahu wants to ‘create conditions’ for Iran regime change
stories.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said he wants his country’s attacks on Iran to help bring about regime change.

stories.title: Iran team hit back at Trump over FIFA World Cup exclusion threat
stories.description: Iran says no one can exclude it from the FIFA World Cup 2026, which is co-hosted by United States, Canada and Mexico.

stories.title: Sri Lanka to repatriate remains of 84 Iranian sailors killed in US attack
stories.description: The 32 sailors who were rescued by Sri Lanka's navy will remain in the island nation, said the Foreign Ministry.

stories.title: Explosions near Tehran al-Quds Day march in solidarity with Palestinians
stories.description: State media outlet Press TV says one woman was killed from shrapnel as a result of a US-Israeli airstrike.

stories.title: Deadly blast rocks Iran as leaders rally in show of defiance
stories.description: Deadly explosions rocked Tehran close to a pro-government rally attended by top officials on Friday, as Israel and Iran unleashed fresh strikes in a war that has ignited the Middle East and threatens to torpedo the world economy. Since erupting on February 28 with US-Israeli atta...

stories.title: Breakdown of diplomacy further complicates Iran nuclear issue, Pakistan warns Security Council
stories.description: Pakistan told the UN Security Council on Thursday that the breakdown of diplomacy in the Middle East has further complicated the Iranian nuclear issue, as Secretary-General António Guterres warned that the escalating crisis poses a “grave threat to international peace and securit...

stories.title: Assailant dead after ramming vehicle into Michigan synagogue
stories.description: A 41-year-old man was killed on Thursday after ramming his pickup truck into a synagogue on the outskirts of Detroit, Michigan, causing a blaze and triggering a huge police response. US media reported the assailant had recently lost family members in an Israeli strike in Lebanon,...

stories.title: Oil market becomes hostage to Iran war
stories.description: TEHRAN: Rescue workers gather as residents collect their belongings from the rubble of a residential building, damaged by US-Israeli strikes.—AFP • IMO to weigh Hormuz threats at extraordinary session as US Navy ‘not ready to escort ships’• Iran denies mining strait, offers passa...

stories.title: Strike on Beirut kills 12 after Israel’s ‘land grab’ threat
stories.description: SMOKE rises after Israeli air strikes target buildings in Beirut’s central Bachoura neighbourhood, close to the headquarters of the Lebanese government.—Reuters • 28 hurt in seafront attack targeting civilians, displaced families; death toll in Lebanon near 700• Hezbollah retalia...

stories.title: Rahul sees ‘Epstein link’ in Modi’s capitulation
stories.description: NEW DELHI: India has been blowing hot and cold at Iran over the oil flow disruption through the Hormuz and its targeting of US assets in Gulf states, but it is also talking to Tehran to allow its oil to be shipped, reports said on Thursday. The apparent capitulation of Prime Mini...

stories.title: Gunman dead after attack on Michigan synagogue
stories.description: An unidentified shooter was killed on Thursday after exchanging gunfire with security in an attack on a synagogue on the outskirts of Detroit, Michigan, police said. Police said the gunman was dead following the attack on the Temple Israel synagogue in West Bloomfield. Law enforc...

stories.title: US refuelling aircraft crashes in western Iraq: US military
stories.description: An American KC-135 aerial refuelling aircraft crashed in western Iraq while a second plane involved in the incident landed safely, the US military said on Thursday. In a statement, US Central Command said it was carrying out rescue efforts after the US KC-135 refueling aircraft w...

stories.title: In first remarks, Iran’s new supreme leader vows to avenge martyrs, keep Strait of Hormuz closed
stories.description: Iran will ​avenge the blood of its martyrs, keep the Strait of Hormuz closed and attack US bases, new Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba ‌Khamenei said on Thursday in a statement read out on state television, his first remarks since succeeding his father following his assassination...

stories.title: Pakistan backs Bahrain, Russia draft resolutions at UNSC, urges de-escalation
stories.description: WASHINGTON: Pakistan has outlined the rationale behind its votes on two competing draft resolutions at the United Nations Security Council, stating that both were aimed at curbing regional escalation and promoting an immediate return to dialogue. On March 11, the Security Council...

stories.title: Pakistan playing role of ‘bridge builder’ in ongoing Middle East conflict: FO
stories.description: Foreign Office (FO) spokesperson Tahir Andrabi said on Thursday that Pakistan was playing the role of a “bridge builder” during the ongoing US-Israeli war on Iran. The war began as the US and Israel launched strikes on Tehran on February 28, which also resulted in the assassinati...

stories.title: South Korea says it can deter threats from North if US shifts weapons to Middle East
stories.description: SEOUL: South Korea said on Wednesday it could deter threats from the North even if the United States relocated some of its military assets to the Middle East amid the war in Iran. Washington, Seoul’s key security ally, stations about 28,500 troops and a range of air and missile d...

stories.title: Read the full text of Iran’s new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei’s first address
stories.description: Iran’s new Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba ‌Khamenei delivered his first public address on Thursday in the form of a statement read out on state television. Below is a translated transcript of his speech. “Peace be upon you, O you who invite to God and are the divinely guided in...

stories.title: In ‘restricted meeting’ with Saudi crown prince, PM Shehbaz assures him of Pakistan’s ‘full solidarity, support’
stories.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif held a “restricted meeting” with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman on Thursday, PM’s spokesperson for foreign media Mosharraf Zaidi said in a post on X after the premier landed in Jeddah. The statement said the premier expressed Pakistan’s “full...

stories.title: Iran’s uranium enrichment secures independence, peaceful atom — supreme leader
stories.description: Ali Khamenei added that the US and other countries are not interested in Iran's progress and have put forward conditions presupposing a complete halt of uranium enrichment on Iranian territory

stories.title: Tehran could weigh nuclear deal with US with Iran-based enrichment consortium — Axios
stories.description: According to the report, a sixth round of talks on the Iranian nuclear program between the US president’s Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Iran’s top diplomat, Abbas Araghchi, could be held this weekend in a Middle Eastern country

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
